Name: latency_bounded_inference
Title: Optimizing Latency in Neural Networks via PAC-Bayes Frameworks and Spectral Stability
Short Hypothesis: Can we leverage PAC-Bayes frameworks to enforce strict latency constraints in neural networks while maintaining accuracy through a spectral stability approach?
Related Work: Existing literature primarily focuses on either PAC-Bayes frameworks or latency minimization independently. However, there is a lack of work that integrates these two approaches to enforce concrete latency bounds while preserving model performance. This proposal will bridge that gap by using spectral stability bounds to derive enforceable protocols for latency-critical inference.
Abstract: This proposal aims to investigate the intersection of PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability in the context of latency-critical inference in neural networks. We will explore how to define and enforce strict latency budgets while minimizing hidden-state perturbation using a Jacobian-based layer-skipping method. By integrating PAC-Bayes theory with spectral stability, we seek to derive protocols that ensure rigorous latency guarantees without sacrificing accuracy. Our experimental design will include first-class latency benchmarks alongside perturbation-cost and memory-bandwidth accounting, targeting the throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier. We will validate the effectiveness of our proposed methodologies through a series of structured experiments designed to isolate the impact of latency bounds on model performance, thus providing empirical evidence for our hypotheses. The expected contributions of this work include a novel framework for latency-aware neural network design that prioritizes computational tractability and reproducibility, ultimately advancing the state of the art in machine learning inference.
Experiments: 1. Implement a baseline neural network and evaluate its latency and accuracy under traditional training regimes.
2. Apply the proposed Jacobian-based layer-skipping approach to the same network, measuring changes in latency and accuracy.
3. Use PAC-Bayes frameworks to derive strict latency constraints and integrate them into the model training process, documenting the impact on performance.

4. Conduct a comparative analysis of throughput-accuracy trade-offs for different models under varying latency budgets, employing metrics such as latency (in milliseconds), accuracy (in percentage), and throughput (in samples per second).
Risk Factors and Limitations: 1. The complexity of implementing the Jacobian-based layer-skipping may introduce unforeseen bugs or inefficiencies.
2. Strict latency constraints might lead to overfitting if not managed correctly, potentially reducing model generalization.
3. The experimental design requires precise measurement of latency, which can be affected by external factors such as hardware variability.
